source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt: Design the chassis and excavation-arm mount of a regolith mining robot benchmark as a bolted/welded multi-part aluminum assembly consisting of the base chassis plate, two side rails, front cross-beam, rear cross-beam, four wheel drive mount brackets, the excavation arm mount plate (where the active digging mechanism attaches), and a regolith-deposit hopper bracket. Robot stowed envelope must fit within 1.5 m length x 0.75 m width x 0.75 m height at the start of the mission; total robot mass must not exceed 80 kg. Operational arena is a 7.38 m x 3.88 m lunar-analog regolith bin filled with BP-1 simulant (bulk density ~1500 kg/m^3, angle of internal friction 45 deg). Robot must autonomously traverse the arena, excavate >= 15 kg of BP-1 in a 15-minute mission, and deposit it into a collection bin. Chassis material: 6061-T6 aluminum extrusion + plate (Young's modulus 68.9 GPa, Poisson 0.33, yield 276 MPa, ultimate 310 MPa, density 2700 kg/m^3). Excavation arm mount is the bolted interface plate where the active digging mechanism attaches; it must transmit the BP-1 digging reaction load into the chassis without bending the chassis out of kinematic alignment. Load cases: (LC1) BP-1 digging reaction: 500 N horizontal opposing motion + 300 N vertical downforce at the excavation arm tool tip, moment arm 0.4 m from arm-mount bolt pattern - total 680 N at mount with 260 N-m moment; (LC2) 15-minute duty cycle: 100 digging strokes per mission at 1 per 9 s, alternating loading between 0 and full LC1 at 0.11 Hz, 100 cycles; (LC3) traverse impact: 2 g vertical on one wheel crossing a BP-1 berm; (LC4) regolith-laden return: 15 kg BP-1 mass distributed on deposit hopper plus 80 kg robot at 1 g; (LC5) tip/tilt: 15 deg side slope with full regolith load; (LC6) dust-seal test: 5 kPa differential across bearing seals with BP-1 particulate loading. Pass/fail: (R1) max von Mises in chassis and mount <= 184 MPa (yield 276 / 1.5) across LC1-LC5; (R2) arm-mount bolt pattern distortion under LC1 <= 0.3 mm to preserve excavation kinematic accuracy; (R3) under LC2 fatigue, alternating stress at mount fillets <= 55 MPa (aluminum Kt=3 endurance at 10^5 cycles scaled by Miner); (R4) total robot mass <= 80 kg; (R5) stowed envelope compliance 1.5 x 0.75 x 0.75 m verified geometrically; (R6) first chassis torsional mode above 15 Hz. Verification is performed on the assembled chassis + arm-mount plate + hopper multi-body bolted model with representative bolt stiffness; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Deliverables: STEP, FEA per case, fatigue, modal, mass and envelope report.
short_prompt:
Design the chassis and excavation-arm mount of a regolith mining robot benchmark as a bolted/welded multi-part aluminum assembly.

Stowed envelope 1.5 m x 0.75 m x 0.75 m; robot mass <= 80 kg. Arena is a 7.38 m x 3.88 m BP-1 simulant bin (bulk density ~1500 kg/m^3, phi 45 deg). Mission excavates >= 15 kg of BP-1 in 15 minutes. Chassis 6061-T6 aluminum extrusion + plate (E 68.9 GPa, Poisson 0.33, yield 276 MPa, ultimate 310 MPa, density 2700 kg/m^3).

Load cases.
- LC1 BP-1 digging: 500 N horizontal + 300 N vertical at tool tip, 0.4 m moment arm = 680 N at mount with 260 N-m moment.
- LC2 duty cycle: 100 digging strokes at 1 per 9 s, alternating 0 to LC1 at 0.11 Hz, 100 cycles.
- LC3 traverse: 2 g vertical on one wheel crossing a BP-1 berm.
- LC4 regolith-laden return: 15 kg BP-1 on deposit hopper + 80 kg robot at 1 g.
- LC5 tip/tilt: 15 deg side slope with full regolith load.
- LC6 dust-seal: 5 kPa differential across bearing seals with BP-1 particulate.

Arm-mount bolt-pattern distortion under LC1 <= 0.3 mm; first chassis torsional mode > 15 Hz.

prompt.title: Lunabotics regolith mining robot chassis + arm mount (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: 80 kg, 1.5 x 0.75 x 0.75 m stowed BP-1 mining robot: base plate + 2 side rails + 2 cross-beams + 4 wheel mounts + arm-mount plate + hopper bracket.

parts.name: base_chassis_plate
parts.role: flat structural plate forming the robot's base / deck

parts.name: side_rail
parts.role: left / right structural side rail extrusion

parts.name: cross_beam
parts.role: front / rear cross-beam tying the two side rails

parts.name: wheel_drive_mount_bracket
parts.role: one of four wheel drive mount brackets carrying the berm-crossing impact load

parts.name: excavation_arm_mount_plate
parts.role: bolted interface plate transmitting digging reaction (500 N H + 300 N V + 260 N-m) into the chassis

parts.name: deposit_hopper_bracket
parts.role: bracket supporting the deposit hopper carrying 15 kg of BP-1

assembly_envelope.overall_bbox: 1.5 m L x 0.75 m W x 0.75 m H stowed
assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: X forward along traverse, Y transverse, Z up

boundary_conditions.interfaces: base plate bolted to side rails and cross-beams; wheel drive mounts bolted to side rails; arm-mount plate bolted to base plate with representative bolt stiffness; hopper bracket bolted to rear cross-beam
boundary_conditions.support: four wheel contact patches supported in LC1-LC5; dust-seal LC6 idealized at bearing interface

prompt.objective: minimize chassis mass while passing yield, arm-mount distortion, fatigue, and modal gates within 80 kg budget

interfaces.load_path: base plate bolted to side rails
wheel drive mount brackets bolted to side rails
excavation arm mount plate bolted to base plate, transmitting digging reaction
deposit hopper bracket bolted to rear cross-beam

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: Lunabotics mining robots have a bolted chassis with a dedicated arm-mount plate; digging reaction and fatigue load paths explicitly cross the arm-mount bolt pattern and must be modelled as a multi-body bolted assembly.
notes.speculation_caveats: Wheel mount count, hopper bracket placement, and side-rail extrusion profile are speculative; materials, loads, and thresholds inherit the original sample verbatim.
